name: Pork Stew
recipeIngredient: 2 and a quarter ounces Sliced ripe olives,
recipeInstructions: To prepare Pork Stew, cut 5 thin slices from each bell pepper. Reserve slices. Chop remaining bell peppers (about 1/2 cup each). Cook pork, sausage, onion and garlic in a Dutch oven over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until pork is no longer pink. Drain excess fat if necessary. Stir in chopped bell peppers, broth, basil, cilantro and ground red chiles. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat. Cover and simmer 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Stir corn, tomato, squash and olives into the meat mixture. Cook an additional 15 minutes. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). To prepare Corn Bread Topping, mix all topping ingredients in a bowl and beat vigorously for 30 seconds. Pour meat mixture into an ungreased baking dish or shallow casserole. Spread the corn bread topping evenly over the meat mixture, sealing it to the edge of the dish. Arrange reserved bell pepper slices on top. Bake until the topping is golden brown, about 15 to 20 minutes. Serve with Fresh Tomato Salsa.
